corrections.english: A public place where live fish and other aquatic animals are exhibited.
(Perl, _, programming, transitive, past tense only blessed) To turn (a reference) into an object.
corrections.chinese: （Perl 程式設計）將參照轉換為物件

corrections.english: Some kind of divine or supernatural aid, or reward.
(accounting) The skill or practice of keeping books or systematic records of financial transactions, e.g. income and expenses.
A place where a variety of plants are grown, primarily for scientific or educational reasons, but are normally open to the public, and include ornamental plants from around the world.
The scope of someone's responsibility.
(uncountable, countable, as bare noun) Christian worship held at a church; service. [from 10th c.]
(legal, education) A period of time in a child's life during which the child must receive schooling as required by law.
(music) A piece of music for one or more solo instruments and orchestra.
A male member of congress.
(anatomy) A clear mucous membrane that lines the inner surface of the eyelid and the exposed surface of the eyeball or sclera.
(pathology) An inflammation of the conjunctiva, often due to infection.
(transitive) To build or form (something) by assembling parts.
Of a land, suitable or allowable for constructing a building on.
The process of constructing.
Energy made available for consumption or consumed in the form of electricity or electric power; electricity.
(geometry) A triangle having all three sides equal.
The grand end of something, especially of a show or piece of music.
(sports, also used as an adjective) The defeat of a team by a much weaker opponent.
The planet Earth.
Said or written to a person who is celebrating their birthday in order to convey the good wishes of the speaker or writer (literally, "may you have a happy birthday")
A distinct physical object.
Any of species Padda oryzivora of finches, native of Java, but very commonly kept as a cage bird.
(legal) In civil law, a case where two or more people disagree and one or more of the parties take the case to a court for resolution.
(legal) The conduct of a lawsuit.
Having qualities of splendor or royalty.
A phrase used in notices indicating that the smoking of cigarettes, etc., is not permitted in the surrounding area.
(geometry, specifically, of an, angle) Larger than one, and smaller than two right angles, or more than 90° and less than 180°.
(mathematics, geometry) An angle that is greater than 90 degrees and less than 180 degrees.
(geometry) A triangle one of whose angles is obtuse.
(grammar) Related to grammatical first, second, etc. persons.
The meat from a domestic fowl.
Having a shape like a rectangle.
(transitive, mathematics) To simplify an equation or formula without changing its value.
(geometry) Half of the angle formed by a single straight line, equivalent to 90 degrees.
(hand game) A popular children's game using three hand signs representing rock, paper and scissors, frequently used as a tiebreaker.
(geometry) An angle of 360 degrees; a full circle.
The sum of knowledge accrued by scholars; the realm of refined learning.
(maths, geometry) An angle that is equal to 180° (pi radians or two right angles).
A place where treasure is stored safely.
Capable of doing many things competently.
Having varied uses or many functions.
(formal) A zoo, a park where live animals are exhibited.
A section of a ticket, showing the holder to be entitled to some specified accommodation or service, as to a passage over a designated line of travel, a particular seat in a theater, a discount, etc.
A voucher issued by a manufacturer or retailer which offers a discount on a particular product.
Desirable or beneficial fortune.
Greater in number, quantity, or extent.
Of full legal age, having attained majority.
corrections.chinese: 成年的；已達法定年齡的

corrections.english: (education) Of or relating to a subject of academic study chosen as a field of specialization.
(postpositive) (of a key) Based on a major scale, tending to produce a bright or joyful effect.
Equivalent to that between the tonic and another note of a major scale, and greater by a semitone than the corresponding minor interval. (of an interval)
Occurring as the predicate in the conclusion of a categorical syllogism. (of a term)
A person of legal age.
(education, North America, Australia, New Zealand) The principal subject or course of a student working toward a degree at a college or university.
(usually, in the plural) Land that is covered mostly with water, with occasional marshy and soggy areas.
(grammar) The allative case, or a word in that case.

(grammatical case) A case used to indicate movement onto, or to the adjacency of something. In English, this is usually expressed by the prepositions to or onto, as in "to the house," "onto the house." Languages that use the allative case include Basque, Dyirbal, Estonian, Finnish, Hungarian, and Lithuanian.
Clothing.
Endemic syphilis, a chronic disease of skin and tissue caused by infection by a subspecies of the spirochete Treponema pallidum.
(US, slang, dated) Very good.
(chiefly, British) A woman employed to do housework, traditionally coming and going on a daily basis and paid weekly wages.
Noticeably happy and optimistic.
Neither very well nor very poorly.
A team sport whose main objective is to dodge or catch balls thrown by the opposition.
Apparel or clothing, especially when appropriate for a particular occasion, profession, etc.
A Muslim people originally from north-west China who currently reside in Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, and Russia.
"You too, Brutus" or "even you, Brutus"; expressing a recognition of betrayal.
Belonging to the best or top group in a system of classification.
Particularly, such a cleansing of a toilet.
A suffusion of the face with blood, as from fear, shame, modesty, or intensity of feeling of any kind; a blush; a glow.
A toilet that uses water to flush urine and feces through a pipe to another location, typically a sewer main in urban settings or a septic tank elsewhere.
(China, historical, politics) A radical political faction composed of four Communist Party of China officials that came to prominence during the Chinese Cultural Revolution.
(film, photography, television) A screen (often in disc form) placed between a light and an illuminated actor or object in order to diffuse the glare. [from early 20th c.]
(sound engineering) A device used to shield a microphone from extraneous sounds. [from early 20th c.]
(theater) A template inserted over a light source to control the shape of the thrown light. [from early 20th c.]
An Internet search, such as one performed on the Google search engine.
To search for (something) on the Internet using the Google search engine.
(archaic) The front aspect of the neck; the outside of the throat.
(archaic, literary) The inside of the throat; the esophagus, the gullet; (falconry, specifically) the crop or gizzard of a hawk.
A parasitic worm; a fluke, tapeworm, or nematode.
Recognized for its quality.
Of high quality, particularly good.
The final part of a racecourse from the last curve to the finish line.
(figurative, by extension) The final part of a distance or the final effort needed to finish.
Not contestable; indisputable; certain.
Not disputable; not open to question; obviously true
corrections.chinese: 無可爭議的；不容置疑的

corrections.english: Which cannot be refuted; clearly right, incontrovertible, indisputable, irrefutable. [from 16th c.]
corrections.chinese: 無法反駁的；無可爭辯的

corrections.english: (comparable) Cheerful and good-humoured; jolly, merry.
The Chinese mile, a traditional unit of distance equal to 1500 chis or 150 zhangs, now standardized as a half-kilometer (500 meters).
The fruit of this tree.
(colloquial) Accomplished with great skill; deft; hard to compete with.
The characteristic sound made by a cow or bull.
(onomatopoeia) The characteristic lowing sound made by cattle.
A tract of soft, wet ground; a marsh; a fen.
(often, internet slang) The sound of a kiss, to indicate blowing a kiss to someone.
(biblical character) An Old Testament prophet who advised King David.
A en given name, of Biblical origin.
(slang) Sex or other sexual activity, especially if illicit.
the digit 0 (especially in representations of speech)
(Christianity, chiefly, _, Catholicism) Synonym of Mary, mother of Jesus, in particular reference to her grief at the Christ's crucifixion.
A detached or semi-detached building at a hospital or other building complex.
corrections.chinese: （醫院等建築群的）分館；獨立建築

corrections.english: A kind of potion, charm, or drug; especially a love potion intended to make the drinker fall in love with the giver. [from the 16th c.]
(anatomy) The shallow vertical groove running from the nasal septum to the center of the upper lip.
Having four sides.
A swampy, soggy area of ground.
A long-lived evergreen tree, Manilkara zapota, native to the New World tropics.
The edible fruit of this tree.
The soft, edible fruit of various South American trees, including the sapodilla.
(slang) Very good, excellent, awesome, badass.
To give (something) a meaning or an importance.
(Internet) A website.
A toast when drinking, roughly equivalent to cheers.
(informal) Excellent, first-class.
Abbreviation of submarine.
Able to be submerged.
(North America, dated, _, slang) Excellent.
The cardinal number occurring after thirty-six and before thirty-eight.
A tiny woodland arachnid of the suborder Ixodida.
(idiomatic) Very good; of the highest level or quality. [from 1848]
(intransitive) To drive a truck.
The plants in a forest which only reach a relatively low height (such as shrubs and bushes).
Unchallenged and accepted without question.
Without doubt; without question; certain.
Undeniable, obvious.
corrections.chinese: 毋庸置疑的；無可否認的

corrections.english: A part of a hospital, with beds, where patients reside.

test.english: a way of discovering, by questions or practical activities, what someone knows, or what someone or something can do or is like

examples.english: She had to take/do an aptitude test before she got the job.
examples.chinese: 她先接受了能力測試後才得到這份工作。

test.english: to put something through a process in order to discover if it is safe, works correctly, etc., or if something is present in it

examples.english: The manufacturers are currently testing the new engine.
examples.chinese: 生產廠家目前正試驗這種新的引擎。

yes.english: used to give an affirmative answer or to show agreement, acceptance, or willingness

examples.english: “Are you coming?” “Yes, I am.”
examples.chinese: 「你會來嗎？」「是的，我會來。」

yes.english: an answer or decision that shows agreement or acceptance

examples.english: We need a definite yes by Friday.
examples.chinese: 我們需要你在星期五前給予明確的肯定答覆。

closet.english: a small room or enclosed space used for storing clothes or other household items

examples.english: He hung his coat in the closet.
examples.chinese: 他把外套掛在衣櫥裡。

shooter.english: a person who shoots a gun, bow, or other weapon

examples.english: The shooter hit the centre of the target.
examples.chinese: 射手擊中了靶心。

shooter.english: a video game in which the main activity is shooting enemies or targets

examples.english: This online shooter supports teams of five players.
examples.chinese: 這款網上射擊遊戲支援五人隊伍。

abide.english: to accept or tolerate someone or something unpleasant

examples.english: I cannot abide that kind of rude behaviour.
examples.chinese: 我無法容忍那種無禮行為。

brain.english: the organ inside the head that controls thought, memory, feelings, and movement

examples.english: The human brain is extremely complex.
examples.chinese: 人類的大腦極其複雜。

brain.english: an intelligent person who plans or organizes something

examples.english: She is the brain behind the successful project.
examples.chinese: 她是這個成功項目背後的智囊。

jelly.english: a soft, sweet food made with gelatine or a similar substance

examples.english: The children had strawberry jelly for dessert.
examples.chinese: 孩子們以草莓果凍作甜品。

jelly.english: a clear fruit preserve made from fruit juice and sugar

examples.english: She spread grape jelly on the toast.
examples.chinese: 她在多士上塗了葡萄果醬。

snatch.english: to take or grab something suddenly and quickly

examples.english: Someone snatched her bag from her hand.
examples.chinese: 有人從她手中一把搶走了手袋。

snatch.english: a short part of a conversation, song, or period of time

examples.english: I heard a snatch of their conversation.
examples.chinese: 我聽到他們談話的一小段。

trip.english: a journey in which you go somewhere, usually for a short time, and return

examples.english: We are planning a trip to Japan.
examples.chinese: 我們正計劃一次日本之旅。

trip.english: an occasion when someone catches a foot and loses balance

examples.english: A trip on the stairs left him with a bruised knee.
examples.chinese: 他在樓梯上失足，膝蓋因而瘀傷。

trip.english: to catch your foot on something and fall or almost fall, or to cause someone to do this

examples.english: She tripped over a loose cable.
examples.chinese: 她被一條鬆脫的電線絆倒了。

partner.english: a person or organization that you work with or do an activity with

examples.english: She started the company with a business partner.
examples.chinese: 她與一名商業合夥人共同創辦公司。

partner.english: the person you are married to or have a romantic relationship with

examples.english: He attended the event with his partner.
examples.chinese: 他與伴侶一起出席活動。

product.english: something that is made or grown to be sold or used

examples.english: The company launched a new product this week.
examples.chinese: 公司本週推出了一款新產品。

product.english: a result produced by a process, situation, or set of circumstances

examples.english: The plan was the product of months of discussion.
examples.chinese: 這項計劃是數月討論的結果。

product.english: the number obtained by multiplying two or more numbers

examples.english: The product of six and seven is forty-two.
examples.chinese: 六和七的乘積是四十二。

first class.english: of the highest quality or belonging to the best group in a system

examples.english: The hotel provides first-class service.
examples.chinese: 這間酒店提供一流的服務。

examples.english: I am feeling flush after receiving my bonus.
examples.chinese: 收到花紅後，我現在手頭很寬裕。

flush.english: the act of cleaning something by forcing water through it

examples.english: Give the toilet another flush.
examples.chinese: 再沖一次馬桶。

flush.english: a poker hand in which all the cards are of the same suit

examples.english: She won the game with a flush.
examples.chinese: 她以同花贏了這局牌。

good.english: of a high quality or standard

examples.english: This restaurant serves very good food.
examples.chinese: 這間餐廳供應非常好的食物。

examples.english: She is very good at mathematics.
examples.chinese: 她很擅長數學。

examples.english: He is a good person who always helps others.
examples.chinese: 他是一個善良的人，總是幫助別人。

good.english: something that is beneficial or morally right

examples.english: The changes were made for the good of everyone.
examples.chinese: 這些改變是為了所有人的益處。

be.english: used to say that someone or something has a particular identity, quality, or condition

examples.english: She is a doctor.
examples.chinese: 她是一名醫生。

be.english: to exist or be present in a place

examples.english: The keys are on the table.
examples.chinese: 鑰匙在桌上。

examples.english: The meeting will be tomorrow.
examples.chinese: 會議將於明天舉行。

class.english: a group of students who are taught together, or a period when they are taught

examples.english: Our English class starts at nine.
examples.chinese: 我們的英語課九時開始。

class.english: a group of things that share similar qualities

examples.english: These animals belong to the same class.
examples.chinese: 這些動物屬於同一類別。

class.english: a social rank or a level of travel accommodation

examples.english: They travelled in first class.
examples.chinese: 他們乘坐頭等艙。

foot.english: the part at the end of the leg on which a person or animal stands

examples.english: She hurt her left foot.
examples.chinese: 她弄傷了左腳。

foot.english: a unit of length equal to twelve inches or 30.48 centimetres

examples.english: The wall is six feet high.
examples.chinese: 這面牆高六英尺。

examples.english: They stood at the foot of the mountain.
examples.chinese: 他們站在山腳下。

key.english: a shaped piece of metal used to open or close a lock

examples.english: I cannot find my house key.
examples.chinese: 我找不到家門鑰匙。

key.english: the most important thing needed to achieve or understand something

examples.english: Practice is the key to success.
examples.chinese: 練習是成功的關鍵。

key.english: a button on a keyboard or musical instrument that is pressed

examples.english: Press the return key.
examples.chinese: 按下返回鍵。

examples.english: She played a key role in the project.
examples.chinese: 她在項目中擔當關鍵角色。

black.english: having the darkest colour, like the colour of coal

examples.english: He was wearing a black jacket.
examples.chinese: 他穿着一件黑色外套。

black.english: relating to people with dark skin, especially people of African origin

examples.english: The museum celebrates Black history and culture.
examples.chinese: 博物館頌揚黑人歷史與文化。

black.english: the darkest colour, produced by the absence or absorption of light

examples.english: The design is available in black.
examples.chinese: 這款設計有黑色可供選擇。

chest.english: the front part of the body between the neck and the stomach

examples.english: He felt a pain in his chest.
examples.chinese: 他感到胸部疼痛。

chest.english: a large strong box used for storing things

examples.english: The blankets were kept in a wooden chest.
examples.chinese: 毛氈存放在一個木箱裡。

chest.english: a piece of furniture with several drawers

examples.english: Her clothes are in the chest of drawers.
examples.chinese: 她的衣服放在抽屜櫃裡。

dimension.english: a measurement of length, width, height, or another spatial direction

examples.english: Check the dimensions of the room before buying furniture.
examples.chinese: 購買家具前先量度房間的尺寸。

dimension.english: a particular aspect or feature of a situation

examples.english: The decision has an important social dimension.
examples.chinese: 這項決定具有重要的社會層面。

float.english: to stay or move on the surface of a liquid or in the air

examples.english: Leaves floated on the water.
examples.chinese: 樹葉漂浮在水面上。

float.english: to suggest an idea for people to consider

examples.english: She floated the idea of working from home.
examples.chinese: 她提出在家工作的想法供大家考慮。

float.english: a decorated vehicle used in a public parade

examples.english: The parade included several colourful floats.
examples.chinese: 巡遊中有多輛色彩繽紛的花車。

in.english: inside a place, area, or container

examples.english: The documents are in the drawer.
examples.chinese: 文件在抽屜裡。

in.english: during a period of time or after a specified amount of time

examples.english: The train will arrive in ten minutes.
examples.chinese: 火車將在十分鐘後到達。

in.english: inside a place or at home

examples.english: Come in and sit down.
examples.chinese: 請進來坐下。

root.english: the part of a plant that grows underground and absorbs water and nutrients

examples.english: The tree has deep roots.
examples.chinese: 這棵樹有很深的根。

root.english: the basic cause or origin of something

examples.english: We need to find the root of the problem.
examples.chinese: 我們需要找出問題的根源。

root.english: the basic part of a word from which other words are formed

examples.english: The words share the same Latin root.
examples.chinese: 這些詞有相同的拉丁詞根。

strong.english: having great physical power or able to resist force

examples.english: She is strong enough to lift the box.
examples.chinese: 她很強壯，足以提起箱子。

strong.english: having a powerful taste, smell, effect, or concentration

examples.english: This coffee is too strong for me.
examples.chinese: 這杯咖啡對我來說太濃了。

examples.english: She made a strong argument for change.
examples.chinese: 她提出了支持改革的有力論據。

take.english: to get hold of something and move it or carry it with you

examples.english: Please take an umbrella with you.
examples.chinese: 請帶一把雨傘。

take.english: to use a form of transport, route, or course of action

examples.english: We took the train to London.
examples.chinese: 我們乘火車前往倫敦。

take.english: to need a particular amount of time or effort

examples.english: The journey takes about two hours.
examples.chinese: 這段旅程大約需要兩小時。

take.english: to accept, choose, or receive something

examples.english: I will take the blue one.
examples.chinese: 我會選藍色的那一個。

material.english: a substance from which something is made

examples.english: The bag is made from recycled material.
examples.chinese: 這個袋由再造材料製成。

material.english: cloth used for making clothes or other items

examples.english: We chose a soft material for the curtains.
examples.chinese: 我們為窗簾選了一種柔軟布料。

material.english: information or ideas used to create a book, programme, or other work

examples.english: The interviews provided useful material for the article.
examples.chinese: 訪問為文章提供了有用素材。

material.english: relating to physical things, or important enough to affect a decision

examples.english: The new evidence is material to the case.
examples.chinese: 新證據對案件十分重要。

place.english: a particular area, position, or location

examples.english: This is a quiet place to study.
examples.chinese: 這是一個安靜的學習地方。

place.english: a position, seat, or role intended for someone or something

examples.english: Please return the book to its place.
examples.chinese: 請把書放回原位。

place.english: to put something in a particular position

examples.english: She placed the cup on the table.
examples.chinese: 她把杯子放在桌上。

place.english: to recognize or identify someone or something

examples.english: I know her face, but I cannot place her.
examples.chinese: 我認得她的臉，但想不起她是誰。

general.english: affecting or relating to most people, things, or situations

examples.english: There was general agreement about the plan.
examples.chinese: 大家普遍同意這項計劃。

general.english: considering the main features without giving exact details

examples.english: The guide gives a general description of the area.
examples.chinese: 指南對該區作出概括描述。

general.english: a very senior officer in an army

examples.english: The general addressed the soldiers.
examples.chinese: 將軍向士兵講話。

flex.english: to bend a part of the body or tighten a muscle

examples.english: Flex your knees before you jump.
examples.chinese: 跳躍前先屈曲膝蓋。

flex.english: to show off something such as money, skill, or status

examples.english: He likes to flex on social media.
examples.chinese: 他喜歡在社交媒體上炫耀。

flex.english: an act of showing off something impressive

examples.english: Buying that car was a huge flex.
examples.chinese: 買那輛車是一次大肆炫耀。

new.english: recently made, created, discovered, or obtained

examples.english: She bought a new computer yesterday.
examples.chinese: 她昨天買了一部新電腦。

new.english: not experienced in or familiar with a place, activity, or situation

examples.english: I am new to this job.
examples.chinese: 我初來擔任這份工作，尚不熟悉。

new.english: in its original condition and not previously used

examples.english: The shoes still look brand new.
examples.chinese: 這雙鞋看起來仍然嶄新。

examples.english: The temperature fell below zero.
examples.chinese: 氣溫降至零度以下。

zero.english: a point or value from which measurement begins, or a value of nothing

examples.english: Reset the scale to zero.
examples.chinese: 把磅秤重設至零點。

zero.english: having no amount, quantity, or value

examples.english: The account charges zero interest.
examples.chinese: 這個帳戶收取零利息。

attack.english: to use violence against a person, place, or group

examples.english: The animal attacked without warning.
examples.chinese: 那隻動物毫無預警地發動攻擊。

attack.english: to criticize someone or something strongly

examples.english: The report attacked the government's policy.
examples.chinese: 報告猛烈抨擊政府的政策。

attack.english: a sudden occurrence of an illness or strong physical condition

examples.english: He suffered a severe asthma attack.
examples.chinese: 他的哮喘嚴重發作。

deep.english: extending a long way down, back, or inward

examples.english: The lake is very deep.
examples.chinese: 這個湖非常深。

deep.english: serious, intense, or difficult to understand

examples.english: The book raises some deep questions.
examples.chinese: 這本書提出了一些深刻問題。

deep.english: low in sound or rich and strong in colour or flavour

examples.english: He spoke in a deep voice.
examples.chinese: 他以低沉的聲音說話。

beat.english: to hit someone or something repeatedly

examples.english: Rain beat against the window.
examples.chinese: 雨水不斷敲擊窗戶。

beat.english: to defeat someone in a game, competition, or contest

examples.english: Our team beat the champions.
examples.chinese: 我們的隊伍打敗了冠軍隊。

beat.english: a regular movement or sound that forms the basic rhythm of music

examples.english: Dance to the beat of the music.
examples.chinese: 隨着音樂的節拍跳舞。

examples.english: I am completely beat after the long walk.
examples.chinese: 長途步行後我筋疲力盡。

color.english: the appearance that things have as a result of the way they reflect light

examples.english: What color is your car?
examples.chinese: 你的汽車是甚麼顏色？

color.english: to add colour to something or become coloured

examples.english: The children colored the picture with crayons.
examples.chinese: 孩子們用蠟筆為圖畫着色。

color.english: to influence the way someone thinks about or describes something

examples.english: His past experience colored his judgment.
examples.chinese: 過往經驗影響了他的判斷。

little.english: small in size or young in age

examples.english: A little girl opened the door.
examples.chinese: 一個年幼的小女孩打開了門。

little.english: a small amount of something, often less than needed

examples.english: There is little time left.
examples.chinese: 剩下的時間很少。

little.english: to a small degree or not much

examples.english: She slept very little last night.
examples.chinese: 她昨晚睡得不多。

death.english: the end of a person's or animal's life

examples.english: His death was a great loss to the community.
examples.chinese: 他的去世是社區的重大損失。

death.english: the permanent end or destruction of something

examples.english: Many feared the death of the local industry.
examples.chinese: 許多人擔心本地工業的消亡。

ivory.english: the hard white material that forms the tusks of elephants and some other animals

examples.english: The sale of elephant ivory is restricted.
examples.chinese: 象牙買賣受到限制。

examples.english: The walls were painted ivory.
examples.chinese: 牆壁髹成象牙色。

purple.english: having a colour that is a mixture of red and blue

examples.english: She wore a purple dress.
examples.chinese: 她穿着一條紫色連身裙。

purple.english: a colour produced by mixing red and blue

examples.english: Purple is her favourite colour.
examples.chinese: 紫色是她最喜歡的顏色。

silk.english: a fine natural fibre produced by silkworms

examples.english: The scarf is made of silk.
examples.chinese: 這條圍巾由蠶絲製成。

silk.english: soft cloth made from silk fibres

examples.english: The shop sells fine Chinese silk.
examples.chinese: 這間商店售賣優質中國絲綢。

vague.english: not clearly expressed, explained, or understood

examples.english: The instructions were too vague.
examples.chinese: 這些指示太含糊。

vague.english: not clear in shape, detail, memory, or feeling

examples.english: I have only a vague memory of the event.
examples.chinese: 我對那件事只有模糊的記憶。

wing.english: a body part used by birds, insects, or bats for flying

examples.english: The bird injured one of its wings.
examples.chinese: 那隻鳥弄傷了一邊翅膀。

wing.english: a part of an aircraft that provides lift

examples.english: Ice had formed on the aircraft's wings.
examples.chinese: 飛機的機翼上結了冰。

wing.english: a side section of a building or a group within an organization or political party

examples.english: The library is in the east wing.
examples.chinese: 圖書館位於東側翼。

bad.english: unpleasant, harmful, or of poor quality

examples.english: The weather was bad all weekend.
examples.chinese: 整個週末天氣都很差。

bad.english: serious in degree or likely to cause harm

examples.english: He has a bad cough.
examples.chinese: 他咳嗽得很嚴重。

bad.english: not skilled or successful at doing something

examples.english: I am bad at remembering names.
examples.chinese: 我不擅長記住別人的名字。

bed.english: a piece of furniture used for sleeping or resting

examples.english: The child was asleep in bed.
examples.chinese: 孩子在床上睡着了。

bed.english: the ground at the bottom of a river, lake, or sea

examples.english: The river bed was covered with stones.
examples.chinese: 河床鋪滿石頭。

bed.english: an area of ground prepared for growing plants

examples.english: She planted roses in the flower bed.
examples.chinese: 她在花圃種植玫瑰。

bind.english: to tie or fasten something tightly

examples.english: Bind the package with string.
examples.chinese: 用繩把包裹捆好。

bind.english: to impose a legal or moral duty on someone

examples.english: The agreement binds both parties.
examples.chinese: 協議約束雙方。

bind.english: to fasten pages together to make a book

examples.english: The documents were bound into a single volume.
examples.chinese: 文件被裝訂成一冊。

cross.english: a shape or object formed by two lines crossing each other

examples.english: A wooden cross stood on the hill.
examples.chinese: 山上豎立着一個木製十字架。

cross.english: to go from one side of something to the other

examples.english: We crossed the road carefully.
examples.chinese: 我們小心地穿過馬路。

examples.english: She was cross with me for being late.
examples.chinese: 我遲到令她很生氣。

definition.english: a statement explaining the meaning of a word, phrase, or idea

examples.english: Look up the definition in a dictionary.
examples.chinese: 在字典中查閱這個詞的定義。

definition.english: the degree to which an image or sound is clear and detailed

examples.english: The screen displays images in high definition.
examples.chinese: 這個螢幕以高清晰度顯示影像。

eye.english: one of the organs used for seeing

examples.english: She has blue eyes.
examples.chinese: 她有一雙藍色眼睛。

eye.english: an ability to notice or judge visual details and quality

examples.english: He has a good eye for design.
examples.chinese: 他對設計很有眼光。

eye.english: a small hole, especially the hole at the end of a needle

examples.english: Pass the thread through the eye of the needle.
examples.chinese: 把線穿過針眼。

language.english: a system of words and grammar used by people to communicate

examples.english: English is widely used as an international language.
examples.chinese: 英語被廣泛用作國際語言。

language.english: the words and expressions used in speech or writing

examples.english: The report uses simple language.
examples.chinese: 報告使用簡單的措辭。

language.english: a formal system used to write instructions for computers

examples.english: Swift is a programming language.
examples.chinese: Swift 是一種程式語言。

set.english: to put something in a particular position

examples.english: She set the vase on the table.
examples.chinese: 她把花瓶放在桌上。

set.english: to establish a rule, limit, time, or value

examples.english: We set a date for the meeting.
examples.chinese: 我們確定了會議日期。

set.english: a group of similar things that belong together

examples.english: She bought a new set of glasses.
examples.chinese: 她買了一套新玻璃杯。

examples.english: The meal is served at a set time.
examples.chinese: 膳食在預定時間供應。

pitch.english: an area of ground used for playing a sport

examples.english: The players ran onto the football pitch.
examples.chinese: 球員跑上足球場。

pitch.english: how high or low a sound is

examples.english: Her voice rose in pitch.
examples.chinese: 她的聲音音高上升了。

pitch.english: a speech intended to persuade someone to buy or support something

examples.english: He gave a convincing sales pitch.
examples.chinese: 他作了一番有說服力的推銷說辭。

pitch.english: to throw something, especially a ball

examples.english: She pitched the ball to her teammate.
examples.chinese: 她把球投給隊友。

charge.english: to ask someone to pay a particular amount for something

examples.english: The hotel charges fifty dollars for breakfast.
examples.chinese: 酒店早餐收費五十美元。

charge.english: to put electrical energy into a battery

examples.english: I need to charge my phone.
examples.chinese: 我需要為手機充電。

charge.english: an amount of money requested for a service or product

examples.english: There is no extra charge for delivery.
examples.chinese: 送貨不收額外費用。

charge.english: a formal accusation that someone has committed a crime

examples.english: He denied the charge of theft.
examples.chinese: 他否認盜竊控罪。

round.english: shaped like a circle or a ball

examples.english: They sat around a round table.
examples.chinese: 他們圍坐在一張圓桌旁。

round.english: one stage in a competition or series of events

examples.english: She reached the final round of the contest.
examples.chinese: 她晉身比賽的最後一輪。

round.english: a regular series of visits or activities

examples.english: The doctor is doing her morning rounds.
examples.chinese: 醫生正在進行早上的例行巡房。

cast.english: all the actors performing in a film, play, or show

examples.english: The film has an excellent cast.
examples.chinese: 這部電影有出色的演員陣容。

cast.english: a hard covering used to protect a broken bone while it heals

examples.english: His arm was in a cast for six weeks.
examples.chinese: 他的手臂用石膏固定了六星期。

cast.english: to choose an actor for a particular role

examples.english: She was cast as the lead character.
examples.chinese: 她獲選派飾演主角。

cast.english: to throw something in a particular direction

examples.english: He cast the fishing line into the river.
examples.chinese: 他把魚線拋進河裡。

save.english: to stop someone or something from being harmed or destroyed

examples.english: The firefighters saved the family.
examples.chinese: 消防員救了這家人。

save.english: to keep money or avoid wasting time, money, or resources

examples.english: We are saving money for a holiday.
examples.chinese: 我們正在儲蓄金錢去旅行。

save.english: to store computer data so that it can be used later

examples.english: Remember to save the document.
examples.chinese: 記得儲存這份文件。

save.english: an action that prevents an opponent from scoring in sport

examples.english: The goalkeeper made a brilliant save.
examples.chinese: 守門員作出了一次精彩撲救。

case.english: a particular situation or example of something

examples.english: This is a clear case of discrimination.
examples.chinese: 這是一個明顯的歧視事例。

case.english: a matter being considered by the police or a court

examples.english: The police are investigating the case.
examples.chinese: 警方正在調查這宗案件。

case.english: a container used to hold or protect something

examples.english: Keep the camera in its case.
examples.chinese: 把相機放在保護套內。

case.english: an occurrence of a particular illness

examples.english: The hospital reported two new cases.
examples.chinese: 醫院報告了兩宗新病例。

crack.english: a narrow line where something has split without completely breaking

examples.english: There is a crack in the wall.
examples.chinese: 牆上有一道裂縫。

crack.english: a sudden short and sharp sound

examples.english: We heard a loud crack of thunder.
examples.chinese: 我們聽到一聲響亮的雷鳴。

crack.english: to break without separating completely, or to make something do this

examples.english: The ice cracked under his feet.
examples.chinese: 他腳下的冰裂開了。

crack.english: to solve a difficult problem or discover a secret code

examples.english: The team finally cracked the code.
examples.chinese: 團隊終於破解了密碼。

draw.english: to make a picture using a pen, pencil, or similar tool

examples.english: The child drew a picture of her house.
examples.chinese: 孩子畫了一幅她家的圖畫。

draw.english: to pull something in a particular direction

examples.english: He drew the curtains across the window.
examples.chinese: 他拉上窗簾。

examples.english: The exhibition drew a large crowd.
examples.chinese: 展覽吸引了大批人群。

draw.english: a game or competition that ends with equal scores

examples.english: The match ended in a draw.
examples.chinese: 比賽以平局結束。

flat.english: level and smooth, without raised or curved areas

examples.english: The land here is completely flat.
examples.chinese: 這裡的土地完全平坦。

flat.english: containing little or no air, especially in a tyre

examples.english: We had a flat tyre on the way home.
examples.chinese: 我們回家途中有一個輪胎洩了氣。

examples.english: Her performance felt rather flat.
examples.chinese: 她的表演顯得相當平淡。

flat.english: a set of rooms for living in, usually on one floor of a building

examples.english: They rent a small flat in London.
examples.chinese: 他們在倫敦租了一個小公寓。

issue.english: an important subject or problem for discussion

examples.english: We need to discuss this issue today.
examples.chinese: 我們今天需要討論這個問題。

issue.english: one of a series of newspapers, magazines, or journals

examples.english: The article appears in the latest issue.
examples.chinese: 這篇文章刊登在最新一期。

issue.english: to officially provide or announce something

examples.english: The government issued a warning.
examples.chinese: 政府發出了一項警告。

issue.english: to supply or distribute something for use

examples.english: New identity cards will be issued next month.
examples.chinese: 新身分證將於下月配發。

list.english: a series of names, words, or items written one after another

examples.english: I made a list of things to buy.
examples.chinese: 我列了一張購物清單。

list.english: to write or include items in a list

examples.english: The guide lists several local restaurants.
examples.chinese: 指南列出了幾間本地餐廳。

list.english: to lean to one side, especially of a ship

examples.english: The damaged boat began to list.
examples.chinese: 受損的小船開始傾斜。

range.english: the limits between which an amount, number, or quality varies

examples.english: The price range is quite wide.
examples.chinese: 價格範圍相當廣。

range.english: a group of different products or things of the same general type

examples.english: The shop sells a wide range of books.
examples.chinese: 這間店售賣一系列不同的書籍。

examples.english: They crossed a high mountain range.
examples.chinese: 他們越過了一座高山脈。

range.english: to include different things or vary between particular limits

examples.english: The courses range from beginner to advanced.
examples.chinese: 課程涵蓋初級至高級程度。

resolution.english: a formal decision made by a group or organization

examples.english: The council passed the resolution unanimously.
examples.chinese: 議會一致通過這項決議。

resolution.english: the act of solving a problem or ending a disagreement

examples.english: Both sides are seeking a peaceful resolution.
examples.chinese: 雙方都在尋求和平解決方法。

resolution.english: a firm decision to do or not do something

examples.english: My New Year's resolution is to exercise more.
examples.chinese: 我的新年願望是多做運動。

resolution.english: the amount of detail shown in a digital image or display

examples.english: This camera records video at a higher resolution.
examples.chinese: 這部相機以較高解像度錄影。

sense.english: one of the physical abilities to see, hear, smell, taste, or touch

examples.english: Dogs have a very keen sense of smell.
examples.chinese: 狗有非常敏銳的嗅覺。

sense.english: a feeling or awareness about something

examples.english: I had a sense that something was wrong.
examples.chinese: 我感覺到有些事情不對勁。

sense.english: a particular meaning of a word or expression

examples.english: The word has several different senses.
examples.chinese: 這個詞有幾個不同的含義。

sense.english: to become aware of something without being directly told

examples.english: She sensed his disappointment.
examples.chinese: 她察覺到他的失望。

sharp.english: having a thin edge or point that can cut or pierce

examples.english: Be careful with that sharp knife.
examples.chinese: 小心那把鋒利的刀。

sharp.english: sudden and large in amount or degree

examples.english: There was a sharp rise in prices.
examples.chinese: 價格急劇上升。

sharp.english: quick to notice, understand, or react

examples.english: She has a sharp mind.
examples.chinese: 她頭腦敏銳。

sharp.english: clear, distinct, and easy to see or hear

examples.english: The photograph is sharp and detailed.
examples.chinese: 這張照片清晰而細緻。

break.english: to separate into pieces or stop working because of damage

examples.english: Be careful not to break the glass.
examples.chinese: 小心不要打破玻璃杯。

break.english: to fail to obey a law, rule, promise, or agreement

examples.english: He was fined for breaking the law.
examples.chinese: 他因違反法律而被罰款。

break.english: a short period when you stop an activity to rest

examples.english: Let's take a ten-minute break.
examples.chinese: 我們休息十分鐘吧。

examples.english: There was a break in the conversation.
examples.chinese: 談話出現了一段短暫中斷。

copy.english: something made to be the same as an original

examples.english: Please keep a copy of the receipt.
examples.chinese: 請保留收據副本。

copy.english: one example of a book, newspaper, document, or other printed work

examples.english: I bought a copy of the magazine.
examples.chinese: 我買了一本雜誌。

copy.english: to produce something that is the same as an original

examples.english: Copy the file to another folder.
examples.chinese: 把檔案複製到另一個資料夾。

copy.english: to behave, dress, or act in the same way as someone else

examples.english: Young children often copy their parents.
examples.chinese: 幼兒經常模仿父母。

drive.english: to operate and control a motor vehicle

examples.english: She drives to work every day.
examples.chinese: 她每天駕車上班。

drive.english: to cause someone to act in a particular way

examples.english: Ambition drove him to work harder.
examples.chinese: 抱負驅使他更加努力工作。

examples.english: It is a two-hour drive to the coast.
examples.chinese: 駕車到海岸需時兩小時。

drive.english: energy and determination to achieve something

examples.english: Her talent and drive made her successful.
examples.chinese: 她的才華和幹勁令她成功。

fall.english: to move downwards, often quickly and without control

examples.english: The glass fell to the floor.
examples.chinese: 玻璃杯掉到地上。

fall.english: to become lower in amount, level, or value

examples.english: Temperatures will fall tonight.
examples.chinese: 今晚氣溫將會下降。

fall.english: an act of falling to the ground or from a higher position

examples.english: He injured his arm in a fall.
examples.chinese: 他跌倒時弄傷了手臂。

fall.english: a reduction in amount, level, or value

examples.english: The company reported a fall in sales.
examples.chinese: 公司報告銷售額有所下降。

fall.english: the season between summer and winter

examples.english: The leaves change colour in the fall.
examples.chinese: 樹葉在秋天會變色。

parboil.english: to boil food for a short time until it is partly cooked

examples.english: Parboil the potatoes before roasting them.
examples.chinese: 烤馬鈴薯前先把它們煮成半熟。
